svg.title: GIS Analysis Workflow: Measuring Urban Greening in Medellín, Colombia
svg.desc: Five-stage pipeline: Data Collection, Cloud Masking and Compositing in Google Earth Engine, Zonal Statistics in ArcGIS Pro, Statistical Analysis, and Output and Findings for a study of the Siembra Aburrá greening initiative in Medellín, Colombia using Sentinel-2 NDVI time series.
svg.text: GIS Analysis Workflow
Measuring Urban Greening in Medellín, Colombia
Siembra Aburrá Initiative · GEOG 360, University of Richmond
1. Data Collection
• Sentinel-2 imagery (10 m bands) via Google Earth Engine
• 6 annual composites spanning 2015–2021 (Jul–Jun)
• 1 year before, 4 years during, 1 year after the initiative
• Neighborhood boundaries for urbanized Medellín
2. Cloud Masking & Compositing
• Built median NDVI composites for each 12-month period
• Iterated cloud-mask parameters after early runs left gaps
• Focused on Medellín's driest months (Dec–Mar) where possible
3. Zonal Statistics
• Computed mean NDVI per neighborhood in ArcGIS Pro
• Reclassified rasters at a 0.2 NDVI threshold
• Summed high-vegetation pixel area per neighborhood, per year
4. Statistical Analysis
• Ran paired t-tests (Excel) vs. the 2015–16 baseline
• Tested both mean NDVI and high-vegetation pixel area
5. Output & Findings
• NDVI rose sharply, dipped in a 2018–19 drought, then leveled off
0.613 Peak NDVI
5 Top-Gaining Areas
Cerro el Volador, El Remanso & more
Eugenia Echavarria · GEOG 360, Environmental Remote Sensing · Dec. 2024
